touches.ai_summary: LinkedIn outreach to Elijah Nelson at ApexInsights regarding sales productivity solutions. No response received.
Cold call attempt to Elijah Nelson. Left voicemail introducing our sales automation platform and requested callback.
Follow-up LinkedIn message to Elijah referencing previous outreach and sharing case study from similar mid-market SaaS company. No response.
Second cold call attempt to Elijah Nelson. Reached voicemail, left message about rep productivity gains at comparable companies.

Brief qualification call with Elijah Nelson. He's newly appointed VP of Sales, exploring tools to help his team scale. Expressed interest in learning more about our automation capabilities for his 800+ person org.

questions_asked.question: What's driving your search for sales productivity tools right now?
questions_asked.answer: I'm three months into this VP role and inherited a team that's still doing a lot of manual work. Looking to modernize our stack.

next_steps: Schedule discovery call for next week

touches.ai_summary: Cold email sent to Elijah with company overview, case studies, and ROI calculator. Elijah responded asking about implementation timeline and support model.

questions_asked.question: What does a typical implementation look like for a company our size?
questions_asked.answer: Usually 4-6 weeks with our Automations Plus Consulting package, includes dedicated CSM and custom workflow design.

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email confirming discovery call scheduled. Elijah confirmed he'll bring implementation lead Peyton Ramirez to the call.
Attempted check-in call before discovery meeting. Elijah unavailable, left voicemail confirming meeting time.

Pre-discovery call with Elijah to align on agenda. Discussed current pain points around manual data entry and forecasting accuracy. He mentioned budget is available but needs exec buy-in from COO Ezra White.

questions_asked.question: Who else needs to be involved in evaluating a solution like this?
questions_asked.answer: Ezra White, our COO, will need to sign off. He's focused on operational efficiency across the board.

questions_asked.question: Can your platform integrate with our existing CRM and support our custom fields?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have native integrations with major CRMs and support custom field mapping during implementation.

next_steps: Full discovery call Wednesday with Peyton included

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call with Elijah and Peyton Ramirez. Deep dive into current workflows, rep productivity metrics, and automation opportunities. ApexInsights has 28 reps struggling with lead prioritization and follow-up cadences. Peyton asked detailed technical questions about data flow and reporting.

questions_asked.question: What metrics matter most when evaluating rep productivity?
questions_asked.answer: Time to first touch, follow-up consistency, and ultimately pipeline velocity. Our reps are spending 40% of their time on admin work.

questions_asked.question: How does the system handle duplicate detection and data cleansing?
questions_asked.answer: Built-in fuzzy matching algorithms with configurable rules, plus manual review workflows for edge cases.

questions_asked.question: What's the learning curve for end users?
questions_asked.answer: Most reps are productive within a week. We provide role-based training and ongoing office hours.

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email with technical docs, ROI calculator personalized for ApexInsights, and demo scheduling link. Elijah confirmed demo for next Tuesday.
Check-in call attempt to confirm demo prep. No answer, left voicemail.
Brief call with Elijah to prep for demo. He wants to focus on automation workflows and reporting dashboards. Mentioned Ezra will likely want to see this too but wants to preview first.

questions_asked.question: Any specific use cases you want us to showcase?
questions_asked.answer: Lead routing automation and activity tracking. Also want to see how customizable the dashboards are for different sales roles.

next_steps: Customize demo environment with ApexInsights branding

touches.ai_summary: Product demo with Elijah and Peyton. Walked through lead automation, activity tracking, and custom reporting. Both engaged throughout. Elijah particularly interested in forecasting features. Peyton asked about API access and data export capabilities.

questions_asked.question: Can we build custom automations beyond the pre-built templates?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, full workflow builder with conditional logic, plus API access for advanced customization.

questions_asked.question: What's included in the consulting package versus base platform?
questions_asked.answer: Consulting adds custom workflow design, data migration support, and quarterly strategy sessions with a dedicated CSM.

questions_asked.question: How does this compare to what you're doing today?
questions_asked.answer: Night and day. We're cobbling together multiple tools and spreadsheets. This consolidates everything.

next_steps: Arrange exec meeting with Ezra White

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email with demo recording, pricing framework, and technical eval scheduling options. Elijah responded that he wants to run a deeper technical eval before bringing Ezra in.
Email confirming technical eval agenda with Peyton. Shared sandbox environment credentials and evaluation checklist.

Extended technical evaluation with Peyton and Elijah. Deep dive into API documentation, security protocols, data governance, and integration architecture. Peyton tested workflow builder and reporting features hands-on. Some concerns raised about migration complexity from their current patchwork system.

questions_asked.question: What's involved in migrating our historical activity data?
questions_asked.answer: We handle data mapping and transformation as part of consulting package. Typically 2-3 weeks for migration and validation.

questions_asked.question: How do you handle data residency and compliance requirements?
questions_asked.answer: SOC 2 Type II certified, GDPR compliant, with options for regional data storage if needed.

questions_asked.question: Does this architecture meet your security requirements?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, looks solid. I'll need to run it by our IT team for final sign-off but no red flags.

objections: Data migration scope feels uncertain - need clearer timeline and resource commitment from your team
next_steps: Peyton to socialize with IT team
Elijah to brief Ezra before exec meeting

touches.ai_summary: Executive meeting with COO Ezra White and Elijah Nelson. Presented business case focused on operational efficiency and ROI. Ezra challenged assumptions on adoption rates and asked tough questions about TCO vs. incremental hiring. Wants to see aggressive pricing to make the numbers work.

questions_asked.question: What's the realistic productivity gain we can expect in year one?
questions_asked.answer: Based on similar deployments, 20-30% reduction in admin time, translating to 15-20% more selling time per rep.

questions_asked.question: How does this compare cost-wise to just hiring another sales ops person?
questions_asked.answer: Platform scales with your team without linear cost increases. Ops hire is $80-100K annually vs. our per-seat model with enterprise features.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the urgency to solve this now?
questions_asked.answer: We're expanding into new markets next quarter and can't scale our current manual processes. Need infrastructure in place.

objections: ROI timeline needs to be faster - can't justify this investment if payback is beyond 12 months
next_steps: Send formal proposal with multiple pricing scenarios
Elijah to build internal business case

touches.ai_summary: Proposal call with Elijah to review pricing options. Walked through three tiers: 28 seats at standard pricing ($32K ACV), volume discount scenario ($28K), and consulting bundle option. Elijah transparent that Ezra is price-sensitive and may push back on consulting add-on.

questions_asked.question: Can we start with just the platform and add consulting later if needed?
questions_asked.answer: Technically yes, but implementation success rate is significantly higher with consulting included upfront. We can phase the consulting hours.

questions_asked.question: What budget range has Ezra allocated for this initiative?
questions_asked.answer: He hasn't committed a specific number yet. I'm building the case but need to land in the low-to-mid $20Ks to get comfortable approval.

next_steps: Send revised proposal with phased consulting option

touches.ai_summary: Pricing call with Elijah. He's advocating internally but Ezra is pushing for 25% discount to approve. Elijah asked what flexibility exists. Rep offered 15% discount bringing ACV to $27,200 for 28 seats with consulting included.

questions_asked.question: Is there any room to move on price to help me get this across the line?
questions_asked.answer: I can offer 15% off to get to $27,200, which is at the edge of my approval authority for a multi-year commitment.

objections: Ezra's target is closer to $24K - the 15% discount doesn't get us there
next_steps: Elijah to re-engage with Ezra on revised number
Explore multi-year contract for additional discount

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call with Elijah. He's feeling pressure to close this quickly as new VP but Ezra won't budge above $24K. Discussed multi-year contract and reduced consulting hours as levers. Elijah concerned about long-term commitment given he's still new to the role. Rep proposed 23.4% discount to $24,400 with annual contract but full consulting package to ensure his success.

questions_asked.question: Would a two-year deal at a lower annual price make the business case stronger for Ezra?
questions_asked.answer: Maybe, but honestly I'm hesitant to lock us in for two years when I'm still ramping. Prefer annual with option to expand.

questions_asked.question: If we land at $24K, does that include the full consulting package or is that stripped down?
questions_asked.answer: At $24,400 I can keep the full consulting package intact. Below that we'd need to reduce implementation support hours.

objections: Uncomfortable with multi-year commitment given I'm only three months into this role and still evaluating what the team needs long-term
next_steps: Send formal quote at $24,400 with full consulting, annual term
Elijah to present to Ezra this week

touches.ai_summary: Email sent with formal quote: $24,400 ACV, 28 seats, Automations Plus Consulting package, annual contract. Elijah acknowledged receipt and said he's meeting with Ezra tomorrow.
Pricing check-in call with Elijah post-Ezra meeting. Ezra approved at $24,400 but asked about payment terms. Elijah wants to confirm we can do quarterly billing instead of annual upfront.

questions_asked.question: Can we structure this as quarterly payments rather than annual upfront?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can do quarterly billing at this price point. I'll update the contract terms.

next_steps: Send updated contract with quarterly payment terms

touches.ai_summary: Final negotiation call to review contract terms. Elijah confirmed quarterly billing structure works. Discussed implementation timeline - wants to kick off within 2 weeks. Both parties aligned on terms and ready to execute.

questions_asked.question: What's your ideal timeline for getting this signed and implementation started?
questions_asked.answer: Hoping to have contract signed by end of week and kick off implementation first week of next month.
